title: Quiet Rules of the Train
author: Unknown
variants:
There are rules to this train, though no-one will tell you them.

Don't open a chest you've already opened.
Don't follow the same passenger for too long.
When the lanterns dim twice in a row, keep walking.

These are the rules I have made for myself.
They may not be yours.
But you will make your own, soon enough.

Rule 71
Be careful what you eat, not every piece of meat is as good as Rat.

Rule 108
If a stranger asks your name, ignore them.

Rule 96
Be mindful of the skulls, some still habour feelings.

Rule 327
Don't double cross back. If you turn back, keep going back.

Rule 5
Trust the lanterns more than the maps. The maps are old.

Rule 44
Footsteps behind you in an empty carriage? Walk faster. Never run.

Rule 211
If you see your own face on another passenger, don't acknowledge them. They haven't realised yet.

Rule 401
Don't whistle after the lanterns dim. Something else whistles back.

Rule 323
Only those who wish to reach the one who built the train say "@Dev". For the chosen few, it will summon the great creator.

Rule 17
The train keeps the list of what you came here to do, even after you have lost it.

"Press {key.advancements}" they say. But what does that mean? What is "{key.advancements}"? How is it pressed? Press against what? Pressed how???

Rule 205
Show respect to all adventurers upon first meeting. Either bow, crouch, or give gifts.

Win an adventurer fully and they change. They greet you across a carriage. The kind press gifts into your hands unasked. Some follow you. Some fight at your side, and do not stop until the thing that threatened you is down.

Rule 29
Not every adventurer is a friend. Some travel beside you; some draw steel at the sight of your face. You cannot tell which by looking. You learn by living.

Rule 63
A gift always warms an adventurer to you. Give better than they already carry and it warms them more. A spare sword has bought me more friends than kind words ever did.

Rule 140
Stand between an adventurer and what attacks them. They remember who drew a blade in their defence, and repay it.

Rule 88
Cross the carriages beside an adventurer. Ride together long enough and something settles between you. It is the slowest road to a friend, and the surest.

Rule 174
Strike an adventurer and they will turn on you — but not forever. Bow again, and keep bowing. A wound you dealt can be mended by the one who dealt it.

Rule 256
An adventurer watches who you strike. Cut down one they love and they will never forgive it. Cut down one they hate, and watch them warm to you.

Rule 312
Draw your blade near a frightened adventurer and they flee; near a brave one and they charge. The same steel, two ends. A retreat is not peace.

Rule 156
Worn steel is not spent steel. Set a failing piece beside its twin and the two mend into one — blade or plate, the same trick holds.

The mend keeps whatever you laid on the left. Lay your finest there. The right is only fuel.

Treat other passengers well.
It will serve you well
in your next life.

The echoes remember.

The echoes of our past
reflect past lives back at us.

Be kind.

Perhaps you can
befriend it.
